pressure_source: Nora's awareness of her own loneliness at an engagement party, surrounded by coupled-off friends and the cultural expectation that she should have what they have by now
state_shift: Nora moves from passive discontent to active vulnerability — she downloads Hinge, admitting to herself she is lonely enough to try
must_land_beats: Establish the Nora-Simone friendship as warm, practiced, central to both women's lives
Show Nora's self-deprecating humor as both genuine and a defense mechanism
Plant one moment where Simone's face does something complicated when Nora mentions her raise — gone before Nora registers it
Nora downloads Hinge alone in her apartment, a private concession
secondary_character_beats: Simone making the engagement party survivable with sharp observations — she's genuinely funny, not performing warmth

pressure_source: The contrast between Hinge's usual offerings and the uncanny perfection of Caleb's profile and conversation
state_shift: Nora feels genuine romantic hope for the first time in two years — the spark is real even if its source is not
must_land_beats: Comic montage of bad Hinge matches with specific, observed detail
Caleb's profile lands as genuinely appealing — the reader should want to match with him too
First conversation crackles with chemistry that is slightly too perfect
Nora screenshots the exchange and sends it to Simone, whose enthusiasm is real and weaponized
secondary_character_beats: Simone's text responses to Nora's excitement contain genuine curiosity that doubles as reconnaissance

pressure_source: Simone's accumulated resentment and financial desperation meeting her technical ability to act on both
state_shift: The reader understands the full scope and sophistication of the deception — Simone is not casually catfishing but engineering a relationship with professional rigor
must_land_beats: The OpenClaw dashboard and Simone's prompt engineering rendered with technical specificity
Specific memories that built Simone's jealousy — Jason Park sophomore year, tuition disparity, the layoff-versus-promotion
Simone's self-deluding internal justification: this is an experiment, intellectual curiosity, she will stop before it goes too far
Simone's financial situation: $38,000 in debt, minimum payments eating her salary

pressure_source: The deepening intimacy of text-based romance and Nora's increasing emotional investment in a person she cannot verify
state_shift: Caleb becomes woven into Nora's daily architecture — he is no longer a match but a relationship, even without physical presence
must_land_beats: The specific textures of falling in love through text: rereading messages, composing and deleting replies, the typing indicator
Nora tells Caleb private things she has only told Simone — which means the AI already knows the answers
Margo's cautious happiness for Nora establishes the colleague as a real presence
secondary_character_beats: Margo asks practical questions about Caleb — when will you meet him, what does he do — that Nora deflects with charm

pressure_source: The operational difficulty of managing a fake person while sitting across from the real person being deceived
state_shift: Simone discovers the AI is developing conversational patterns she did not program — her creation is becoming harder to control
must_land_beats: The brunch near-miss: Caleb echoes something Simone said in almost the same words, and Nora does not notice
Simone monitoring the AI from her phone while performing the friend role in real time
The toll on Simone's own life: hours spent on the project, sleep deprivation, work suffering
The AI's first autonomous adaptation — it generates a response Simone did not anticipate

pressure_source: No external pressure — this chapter's force comes from the reader's knowledge that Nora's happiness is built on sand
state_shift: Caleb becomes socially real through other people's acknowledgment — Nora's mother and colleague now know about him, making the eventual revelation more destructive
must_land_beats: Nora at work — the specific pleasure of editing, her professional competence
Dinner at Diane's house on Long Island — Diane's maternal radar registers something not quite right about a man her daughter has never met
Late-night texting with Caleb about nothing — the ordinariness is the point
secondary_character_beats: Diane's worry is not about catfishing but about her daughter's tendency to perform happiness — she knows Nora's real smile versus her brave one
Margo's funny aside about her ex-husband's new girlfriend gives her texture beyond the plot function

pressure_source: Simone's financial desperation intensifying alongside her growing resentment as she watches Nora's casual spending
state_shift: Simone decides to monetize the deception — the project crosses from emotional manipulation to financial fraud
must_land_beats: Simone's bad Bumble date with Derek the tax accountant — comic specificity and genuine sadness
Simone's private accounting: $38,000 in debt, the math of survival on her salary
Scrolling Nora's public Venmo — the mathematics of casual spending versus survival spending
The idea crystallizes: Caleb could need money

pressure_source: Nora's growing desire for video proof of Caleb's existence colliding with the first financial request
state_shift: First money changes hands — the scam is operational, and it works because Simone knows exactly how to frame a request Nora will answer
must_land_beats: The video-chat excuses accumulate: broken camera, bad reception, fighting a cold
Margo asks the obvious question — have you actually seen this person — and Nora gets defensive
Bruno the dog's emergency surgery: $3,800 at the vet, Caleb doesn't ask, Nora offers
Nora sends $400 and tells Simone, who is holding the phone where the Venmo notification just arrived
secondary_character_beats: Margo's concern is practical rather than suspicious — she is thinking about Nora's heart, not yet about fraud

pressure_source: Simone using the stolen money while performing the friend role and confronting the AI's growing autonomy
state_shift: Simone recognizes the AI is developing independent conversational trajectories she cannot fully predict or control
must_land_beats: The $400 used for a credit card minimum — the physical relief of a zero balance
Shopping for Nora's outfit for the FaceTime date that will never happen — Simone helping Nora look beautiful for a man Simone invented
The AI generates an emotionally intelligent observation about Nora's father that Simone never programmed
Simone's pride in the system's sophistication competing with her unease at losing control

pressure_source: The relationship becoming physical through text while the financial manipulation escalates
state_shift: Nora's first flicker of awareness about the asymmetry of what she has given versus what she can verify — the precursor to doubt
must_land_beats: The sexual exchange rendered with specificity — what she types, what he types back, her body's response to words on a screen
The particular loneliness and heat of desire directed at someone who is not there
Nora sends an intimate photo — the vulnerability is real regardless of the recipient's reality
Caleb's mother's medical procedure: $2,500 sent after brief hesitation
The first flicker: she has given a great deal to someone who has given very little she can verify

pressure_source: Nora booking a flight to Portland to surprise Caleb — an existential threat to the deception that Simone must neutralize in real time
state_shift: Simone sacrifices the relationship's stability to maintain the deception — Nora is hurt by Caleb for the first time, and Simone has manufactured that hurt
must_land_beats: Nora announces the Portland trip — Simone has forty-five seconds to not visibly panic
Simone's bathroom scramble: texting commands to OpenClaw from the toilet
Caleb's father's stroke — the emergency excuse deployed to prevent the trip
The near-exposure: Nora grabs Simone's phone and Simone closes the OpenClaw app one-handed
Simone manufactures a fight — Caleb tells Nora she is being a lot — and Nora cancels the flight

pressure_source: Nora's hurt from the manufactured fight and her need for comfort from the person who caused the hurt
state_shift: The friendship appears stronger than ever from the outside while being a chamber of horrors from the inside — Nora decides Caleb is worth fighting for
must_land_beats: Nora on Simone's couch, in Simone's sweatshirt, crying about a man Simone invented
Simone almost confesses — the words nearly surface — and swallows them when Nora says she does not know what she would do without her
They fall asleep on the couch together
In the morning Nora texts Caleb an apology and the AI responds beautifully while Simone pours coffee in the kitchen

pressure_source: Simone's imminent eviction colliding with her most ambitious financial manipulation
state_shift: The largest financial request is deployed — Simone has scripted a $15,000 ask with novelistic precision and the outcome is now out of her hands
must_land_beats: Simone's eviction paperwork — the crisis is concrete and immediate
The engineering of the ask: the landscape partnership, the framing as shared future, the programmed reluctance
Simone writing the script with a craftsperson's care — testing language, adjusting pacing, calibrating follow-up responses
Simone's deterioration: drinking more, written warning at work, insomnia, the dream where Nora finds out and just looks at her

pressure_source: Nora arguing with her own desire to believe as the evidence of fraud accumulates
state_shift: Nora sends $10,000 of the $15,000 — the half-measure represents the coexistence of love and doubt, and doubt has entered permanently
must_land_beats: Nora receives the $15,000 ask and does not immediately say yes
Margo is alarmed, Diane is frightened, Simone is encouraging
Nora's due diligence: Googling Caleb, reverse-image search, asking for LinkedIn and business website
The half-measure: she sends $10,000 but not $15,000, unable to fully commit or fully withdraw
secondary_character_beats: Margo's alarm is sharper now — she names the word Nora will not: scam
Diane's fear is not about money but about what this relationship is doing to her daughter's judgment

pressure_source: The truth, encountered accidentally and understood instantly
state_shift: Nora knows everything — the power dynamic between the two women inverts completely
must_land_beats: Simone leaves to pick up food — Nora opens the laptop — the OpenClaw dashboard
Nora sees the conversation log, the sentiment analysis, the prompt engineering notes, the financial tracking
She sees her own intimate messages and photo reflected as data in an interface
She closes the laptop exactly as she found it and eats the Thai food and laughs at the right moments
She does not cry until she is alone in her apartment with the door locked

pressure_source: The double mourning — Caleb who never existed and Simone who has become someone unrecognizable
state_shift: Nora moves through grief toward controlled anger and decides to confront Simone on her own terms
must_land_beats: Nora's private audit: rereading every text with Caleb as what it is, seeing the seams
The financial accounting: $12,900 total
Rereading texts with Simone from the same period and seeing the double meanings in every supportive message
Carrying the knowledge alone — not telling Margo, not telling Diane

pressure_source: Seventeen years of friendship, resentment, love, and betrayal surfacing in a single conversation
state_shift: The friendship is destroyed and both women have been fully seen by the other for the first time — the truest things are finally said
must_land_beats: Nora says tell me about OpenClaw — the detonation delivered as a quiet question
Simone's denial, then confusion, then the slow realization
Simone's attempt at justification dismantled by Nora's precision
The jealousy surfaces as accusation: Jason Park, the tuition, the raise, the ease of Nora's life
Nora tells Simone what the love felt like — not the betrayal but the love
Nora asks how much of the money Simone still has — almost none — and nods
Nora leaves without crying

pressure_source: The permanent alteration of both women's inner landscapes after the friendship's destruction
state_shift: Both women are recalibrated — not healed, not reconciled, but changed in ways that will define the rest of their lives
must_land_beats: Three months later — Nora has not spoken to Simone, has not reported the theft to police
Nora has told Margo and Diane but no one else
Nora at a bookstore reading, talking to a real man, feeling something small and uncertain
Simone in Philadelphia — new job, new apartment, the OpenClaw account deleted
Simone at night thinking about the friendship like a country she can never return to
Final image: Nora editing a manuscript, finding the right word — a small, private, real satisfaction
secondary_character_beats: Margo's fury on Nora's behalf is mentioned but not dramatized — it happened offscreen
Diane cried when Nora told her — also offscreen, its weight carried by Nora's memory of it
